originalAlarm.description: `__buffer_2089` could be null (null value originating from line 2423) and is dereferenced.

sanitizedDescription: `buffer` could be null (null value originating from line 2423) and is dereferenced.
presenceCondition:
(let ((a!1 (or (and (and (not CONFIG_KGDB) (not CONFIG_XMON) CONFIG_BDI_SWITCH)
                    CONFIG_KPROBES)
               (and (and (not CONFIG_KGDB) CONFIG_XMON) CONFIG_KPROBES)
               (and CONFIG_KGDB CONFIG_KPROBES))))
  (and (or (and (not CONFIG_KGDB) (not CONFIG_XMON) CONFIG_BDI_SWITCH)
           (and (not CONFIG_KGDB) CONFIG_XMON)
           CONFIG_KGDB)
       a!1))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_BDI_SWITCH () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_KPROBES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_KGDB () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_XMON () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 221ac329e93.c L41: buffer null pointer dereference (// ERROR at L37). When KGDB, XMON, or BDI_SWITCH is defined, allocate_buffer() is defined as a macro that calls malloc(4092) with no null check. When CONFIG_KPROBES=T, '*buffer = a' dereferences buffer which could be NULL. PC: ((!KGDB AND !XMON AND BDI_SWITCH) OR (!KGDB AND XMON) OR KGDB) AND CONFIG_KPROBES — simplifies to (KGDB OR XMON OR BDI_SWITCH) AND KPROBES, which exactly captures the else-branch (malloc without null check) AND the dereference. No library macros. Known bug, correctly constrained PC.
